c. To correct acquisition date recorded in the PPE Schedules.

a. Adjust the acquisition dates to the actual completion dates; and

To adjust accumulated depreciation of PPEs erroneously recorded:

Other Infrastructure Assets 6,935,503.24
Road Networks 1,993,403.59

3.15 We recommended and management agreed that:
a) The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and General
Services Officer with complete descriptions and cost segregation of the
road components for road projects.
b) The GSO and accounting unit work closely with the Municipal Engineer to
identify and segregate values of road networks and their components. After
which, the Accounting Unit and GSO shall maintain the required LRNLCs
and LRNPCs for the road networks as shown in Annex D and E,
respectively, of COA Circular 2015-008;
c) The accounting unit prepare adjusting entries to reverse depreciations
provided for the non-depreciable components of the road networks;
d) The accounting unit prepare adjusting entries to correct depreciation of the
depreciable components of the road networks;
e) Management directs the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical
inventory of local roads and furnish the result of the inventory through the
RPCLRN to the accounting unit, and for the GSO to render the RLRN at the
end of every accounting period;
f) The accounting unit provide a separate disclosure on the total road network
system in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
g) The accounting unit be guided by the provisions in COA Circular No. 2015-
008 dated November 23, 2015, in its recording of road networks to avoid
deficiencies in the account.

4.5 We recommended and the management agreed to coordinate with the Provincial
Government for the proper PTR for the said properties and ensure that prior to
the signing of the PTR, management should conduct an inspection of the
properties to verify their existence, completeness, accuracy and valuation.

5.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that cash advances for
operating activities, including travel allowances for non-employees or non-
officials of the LGU, are granted only to bonded officers.

6.6 We recommended that the Accounting Unit classify and record subsequent
travel expenses incurred by non-employees or non-officials under Other
Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (5-02-99-990) to ensure proper
expense classification, transparency, and effective budgeting

6.14 The audit team recommended that:
a.) for travelling expenses and allowances granted as cash advance to non-employees
but are Government Employees, bonding requirement is not applicable, however,
accounting these under the travelling expense account is discouraged;
b.) granted cash advance to employees to cover travelling expenses and allowances of
non-government employees, e.g., participants for sports festival or celebration of
provincial foundation, the bonding requirement applies and accounting these under
the travelling expense account is discouraged.

7.5 We recommended that management strictly enforce the prescriptive period for
liquidation of cash advances, in accordance with EO No. 77 and COA Circular
No. 97-002, to reduce the risk of accumulation of unliquidated and potentially
misused public funds.

8.5 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
1. Strictly require the Revenue Collection Clerks (RCCs) to prepare the Report
of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and remit all collections in full to the
Municipal Treasurer at the close of each business day. For collectors
assigned to field stations where travel to the Treasurer’s Office takes more
than one day, remittances should be made at least once a week or
immediately once collections reach ₱5,000.00, whichever comes first.
2. Regularly monitor accountable forms remaining in the custody of
accountable officers to determine whether they are holding collections that
have not yet been remitted.
3. Carefully check the amounts reported in the RCDs against the actual issued
accountable forms to avoid errors that may result in shortages or overages.

a. ensure that all received and collected monies arising from taxes, revenue, or receipts of any kind are regularly deposited intact;

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Accountant, recognize/take up in the books the transferred properties amounting to  ₱5,766,808.75 to fairly present the balance of the Property, Plant and Equipment and the Equity Account.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the local accountant and the general services officer/supply officer with the complete description and cost segregation of recorded Road Networks in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.

We recommended that management strictly abide by the required procedures of procurement to ensure that the most advantageous price for the government is obtained in consonance with Section 48 of the Revised 2016 IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that:

Management ensure that  all programmed development projects are efficiently implemented in order to maximize the achievement of the desired socio-economic development outcomes by the intended beneficiaries and the general public; and

In the identification of development projects, consider only the priority needs of constituents and ensure that these are well planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready in consonance with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 to ensure smooth project implementation.

We recommended and management agreed that all required reports and relevant information on programs, projects and activities of the agency be submitted to the Audit Team, within timelines set, to enable the publication of updated project status and to promote good governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended that:
Management ensures that the detailed technical specifications on items to be procured are provided in the PRs and the corresponding RFQs to ensure competition and that items actually needed are obtained at the most advantageous prices; and
Management issue RFQs providing the necessary information to guide bidders in the preparation and submission of their bids which are responsive to the needs of the procuring entity.

We recommended that Management, thru the OBO, ensure that no building/structure shall be constructed within the Municipality without first obtaining a building permit to ensure their conformity with approved standard requirements on zoning and land use, lines and grades, structural design, sanitary and sewerage, environmental health, electrical and mechanical safety as well as with other rules and regulations promulgated in accordance with the PD 1096.

We recommended that the Accounting Office and the Supply Officer exert effort to reconcile their records pertaining to the PPE accounts to ensure that the amounts presented in the financial statements are accurate and valid.
observations.managementAction: The PPE accounts were already reconciled

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments in the books to remove the items which are not considered as PPE, in consonance with Section 4.2 of COA Circular No.2020-006 dated January 1, 2020.

We recommended that Management:

Ensure that payrolls are duly approved by the approving authority and duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer;

Submit the lacking DTRs or any document evidencing actual rendition of service by the payees as claimed; and

Require the Municipal Treasurer to release payment directly to the payee or secure authority and identification documents from the claimant, in case payment is made to a representative.

adjustments in the books to remove the items which are not considered as
PPE, in consonance with Section 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020. The recommended adjusting entries are as follows:

• Renew PARs every three years and every time there is a change in
custodianship;
• Ensure that the end-user duly acknowledge receipt of properties by signing
in the “Received from” portion and the Supply Officer sign the “issued
by” portion of the PAR to pinpoint responsibility over the PPE;
• Ensure that the PARs are properly numbered and dated in accordance with
the numbering under Section 58 of GAM for LGUs, Volume II to serve as
controlling measure and to determine when is the next renewal of PARs; and
• Ensure that the ICS is use to issue tangible items which costs ₱15,000.00
and below and has life of more than one year in accordance with Section
59 of GAM for LGUs, Volume II.

4.5 We recommended that management ensure that cash advance for
payment of payrolls is always supported with copies of the duly approved
payrolls to establish validity of claim as well as facilitate verification of the
corresponding liquidation reports.

5.7 We recommended that:
a. For future similar transactions, management ensure that these are
supported with the documents as required under DSWD Memorandum
Circular No. 24, series of 2020;
b. Management ensure that only the approved payrolls for which a cash
advance is drawn are paid out of said cash advance;
c. The disbursing officer refrain from revising the payrolls once approved for
payment and, if there are eligible beneficiaries not included and funds are
still available, prepare a supplemental payroll for approval by proper
authorities; and
d. The disbursing officer ensure that acknowledgment of receipt of claim by
each payee is clearly shown in the payroll and attach authorization letters
of payees whose claims are received by their authorized representatives.

6.7 We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure that payrolls are duly approved by the approving
authority and duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer;
b. Submit the lacking DTRs or any document evidencing actual
rendition of service by the payees as claimed; and
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to release payment directly to the
payee or secure authority and identification documents from the
claimant, in case payment is made to a representative.

7.4 We recommended that management submit the GAD Plan and Budget
and corresponding Accomplishment Report in strict adherence to Item V
of COA Circular No 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

8.6 We recommended that management prepare and submit monthly the
Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Fund every 15th day after the end of each month
as required under Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No 2012-002.

We recommended that management:
a. Cause the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines on disposal of
property;
b. Upon disposal of the properties, the Property and/or Supply Unit submit the Inventory and Inspection
Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to the Accounting Unit, which will be the basis for the dropping of
the properties from the book of accounts and for taking up the proceeds from the sale of the property, if any.

contractors with the same pledged key personnel.

programs, projects and activities to achieve the intended purpose of the fund.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and the Municipal Accountant, identify the road
network where the completed project belongs and make the necessary
adjustments in the book of accounts of the Municipality.

Management’s Comment:
1.8 The Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary adjustment and to
prepare Journal Entry Voucher for the recording of the road networks to the
respective Barangays/Province.

2.7 We recommended that in succeeding transactions, the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC), through the BAC Secretariat, consolidate purchase
requests of similar items approved at or about the same period to determine
the necessity of public bidding.

Management’s Comment:
2.8 The particular transactions weren’t purposely done to evade and violate the rules
and laws on government procurement specifically public bidding; it was an act
of good faith. In fact, management has always been compliant of audit rules,
regulations and recommendations.

2.9 The hardware materials for different barangays were purchased separately
because of the differences in unit prices since the cost of hauling was
incorporated in the material’s unit costs.

2.10 After the receipt of the AOM, PRs of hardware materials within the same
barangay and of the same unit prices are already consolidated.

Auditors’ Rejoinder
2.11 The Audit Team maintains its stand that there was splitting of PRs in these
transactions hence, the necessary audit action will be issued.

3.7 We recommended that Management assign a personnel in charge of the
orderliness, safety and upkeep of the storeroom and the inventories.

Management’s Comment:
3.8 Management has already unloaded the workload of the person-in-charge of the
stockroom for him to concentrate on his/her function as the storage keeper.
Likewise, personnel of the Municipal Engineering Office will assist in the
fabrication of shelves/cabinets for the stocking of inventories.

4.10 We recommended that Management, thru the SWMB:
a. Attend a seminar to further gain knowledge in the process and
management of sanitary landfill facility;
b. Conform with the pertinent provisions of RA 9003 on the maintenance
of SLF and transportation equipment;
c. Construct temporary storage for the recyclable materials deposited in
the disposal area of the SL;
d. Transfer the residual wastes mistakenly dumped in the leachate pool to
the disposal area and cover the same with soil;
e. Provide the needed facilities of the Rural Health Unit and Barangay
Health Stations, such as concrete vaults and proper waste bins;
f. Install security fence around the septic/concrete vault with the required
marking: “CAUTION: HAZARDOUS WASTE OR SHARPS DISPOSAL
AREA- UNATHORIZED PERSONS KEEP OUT”; and
g. Regularly inspect, monitor and maintain the cleanliness inside and
outside the premises of health facilities.

Management’s Comment:
4.11 The SLF is not yet fully operating and due to budgetary constraints, some of the
facilities were not yet available, however management is bent on addressing the
issue. In fact after the receipt of the Audit Observation Memorandum, initial
actions were conducted to address the issue. The residual wastes that were
mistakenly dumped in the leachate pool were already transferred to the disposal
area. Likewise, the recyclable materials in the disposal area were already put in
sacks.

4.12 As for the security fences around the RHU’s septic vaults with marking, these
were already constructed. Likewise, waste bins with proper label were already
provided. Similarly, expired medicines openly dumped in the RHU backyard
were already acted upon.

4.13 On the issue of open burning, this will be coordinated to barangay officials for
proper action.

5.9 We recommended that the engineers involved in the preparation of the
POWs and DEs of the contract include only the cost of mobilization and
demobilization within 1% of the EDC, and stop subjecting the same to OCM
and profit mark up as provided in the DO No. 197, series of 2016.

Management’s Comment:
5.10 The Municipal Engineer admitted the lapses on the preparation of the Program of
Works and committed to abide with the recommendations of the Audit Team.
We recommended that Management seek the required authorization, otherwise, revert the fund to the respective Source Agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fast track its implementation of the PPAs on the prevention, mitigation, and preparedness portion of the LDRRMF for an effective attainment of the goals of disaster management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management determine whether they are authorized to use the savings from the completed projects; otherwise, return the same to the SA. Moreover, we recommended for the return of the PDAF balances to the BTr.
observations.reason: An audit observation was issued for CY 2017 under Part II of this report for unutilized balance not reverted to source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer, strictly monitor and immediately implement programs, projects and activities as budgeted and within the time frame it was planned.
observations.reason: For the 2017 programs, 37% were still on-going and 47% were not yet started as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management seek the required authorization, otherwise, revert the fund to the respective Source Agencies.

We recommended that Management thru the Municipal Accountant to prepare the remittance of the share from S&G taxes of barangay in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular 92-382.

We, further recommended that the unremitted balance be remitted as soon as possible.
We recommended that management book up the replaced but still serviceable tires as inventory.
We recommended that the Local Finance Committee include only attainable estimates in its budget to avoid the unimplemented PPAs for lack of funding.
observations.reason: Collections for CY 2017 of ₱383,538.37 exceeded the estimated collection of ₱363,049.50.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the budgetary requirement provided under PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 on the implementation of Gender and Development programs.
observations.reason: GAD budget for CY 2017 exceeded the 5% of the total budget of the MLGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Finance Committee provide only collection estimates that are attainable. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer innovate strategies that would intensify collections.
observations.reason: Only 82.25% or ₱3,817,949.66 of the target collection of ₱4,641,437.78 was collected for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management should:
➢ Post the citizens charter to guide the clients for the procedures to be followed.
➢ Orient Liason Officers with the required supporting documents for prompt processing of claims.
➢ Adhere to the time frame indicated in its Citizens Charter to ensure efficient delivery of its services.
observations.reason:
Procurement lag was observed that caused delay on implementation of programs

An AOM was issued for the time lag in procurement process that causes delay and their citizen’s charter was not yet updated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the persons liable to settle their audit disallowances thru the issuance of notices and coordination with the agencies where said liable persons are connected, if any.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the disallowance had a balance of ₱359,444.76.

observations.recommendation:
1.6. We recommended that additional staff be provided to the BAC Secretariat
and the Supply Office. Henceforth, contracts/purchase orders, including
the related supporting papers/attachments, will be furnished to the Office
of the Auditor within five (5) days after approval.

2.6. We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Officer submit Monthly Report on the Utilization of Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end
of each month pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that management, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer, strictly monitor and immediately implement programs, projects and activities as budgeted and within the time frame it was planned.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management should revert unspent balance of the 20% municipal development fund for completed and fully paid projects.
observations.managementAction: Management have already reverted the unspent balance from 20% MDF for re-appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management review its policies or qualification of grantees in the similar program. implementation of its Personnel Education Program to be assured that all personnel granted can meet the terms and conditions stipulated in the MoA and results will be responsive to the objectives of the program.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, there was no implementation of its Personnel Education Program. However, management would consider the recommendation in case similar program will be formulated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Finance Committee provide only collection estimates that are attainable. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer innovate strategies that would intensify collections.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury in order to intensify the collection of taxes conducts house to house collection, payroll deduction of RPT on employees, deputation of qualified Brgy. Treasurers, raffle draw for RPT taxpayers and regular sending of taxbills to all agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should:  Post the citizens charter to guide the clients for procedures to be followed.  Orient Liason Officers with the required supporting documents for prompt processing of claims.  Adhere to the time frame indicated in its Citizens Charter to ensure efficient delivery of its services.
observations.managementAction: Citizen’s charters were already posted in each office to guide clients and required documents were also attached. For CY 2016, procurement process of most transactions takes 1 month while processing of claims were done for 1 month to 3 months.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Supply Office and the Accounting Office perform regular reconciliation of their records in order to ensure accuracy or reliability of the PPE balances.
observations.managementAction: The accounting and property records were already reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Supply Officer forward a copy of the Inventory and Inspection Report of unserviceable Unserviceable Property to the Accounting Office for reclassification of PPEs which are unserviceable or no longer used, to the appropriate account - Other Assets.
observations.managementAction: After the audit observation was issued, the Unserviceable properties were reclassified to the Other Assets account. However, it was again reclassified to their original accounts upon the effectivity of the PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality adhere strictly with its 45% Personal Services limitation in accordance with Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: No additional/year-end bonus was granted in excess of PS limitation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere with the limitations in the PEI granting of Performance Enhancement Incentive as provided under Section 1(a) of Executive Order No. 80.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, only the allowed of ₱5,000.00 was granted to each employee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere with the posting of bid security and performance bond as prescribed under the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Contracts submitted for CY 2016 were already attached with the bid and performance securities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure that all contracts and other related documents are duly accomplished to determine compliance with required timelines.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, contracts submitted were already dated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the persons liable to settle their audit disallowances thru the issuance of notices and coordination with the agencies where said liable persons are connected, if any.
observations.managementAction: Management require the monthly deducts from salary of two employees. As of December 31, 2016, the disallowance had a balance of ₱394,440.38.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the LGU provide this office with copies of the following documents for reference and to reflect that the ratio of employment and the employees are based on the actual needs of the programs to be effectively implemented: a. List of Job Order Employees with corresponding functions and office assignments including Organizational Charts
observations.managementAction: Already submitted the required documents.
